description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player featuring a large, faceted golden horn speaker mounted atop a dark reddish-brown wooden base. The base is rectangular with a stepped design and supports a circular black turntable platter, which holds a vinyl record with a red label at its center. A metallic tonearm with a golden head extends over the record, and a hand-crank mechanism is attached to the side, indicating manual operation. The horn is prominent and angular, flaring outward to amplify sound, and is visually striking due to its size and geometric shape. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century gramophones, with a blend of wood, metal, and plastic materials. No dust cover or electronic controls are visible, emphasizing its classic, mechanical aesthetic.
description: A vintage record player with a large golden horn, dark wooden base, black turntable platter, and a hand-crank mechanism. The design is classic and mechanical, with a prominent faceted horn and metallic tonearm.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the large golden horn, the dark wooden base, and the hand-crank on the left side.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the horn's profile, the full length of the tonearm, and the hand-crank extending outward.
description_view_2: The rear view centers the horn's opening, with the hand-crank now on the right and the tonearm visible over the platter.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the horn's side, the tonearm's attachment, and the hand-crank facing forward.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It is slightly raised and supports a vinyl record with a red label. The platter appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a matte finish and no visible grooves or patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and extends from the side of the base over the platter. It is straight with a golden head, medium in length, and appears to be made of metal with a matte silver finish. The tonearm is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready to play.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
